pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh confirm this is the specified channel name for the task

HL7 standard: Port 6661 is the default MLLP (Minimum Lower Layer Protocol) port for HL7 v2 messages per HL7 Transport Specifications. Confirmed in setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 84 and export_result.sh line 9.
Specified in task description, setup_task.sh line 14, and export_result.sh line 8. Directory creation confirmed in setup_task.sh lines 14-20.

Specified in task description, verified in export_result.sh line 75 which checks for this exact header string. Header format matches required CSV columns for patient demographics from HL7 PID segment.

HL7 v2.3 message parsing: Message 1 PID segment parsed and all required fields extracted. MRN from field 3, names from field 5, DOB/gender/address/phone/SSN from subsequent fields. Python parsing verification confirms exact CSV match.
HL7 v2.3 message parsing: Message 2 PID segment parsed and all required fields extracted. Python parsing verification confirms exact CSV match.
MSH segment in both test messages contains '||2.3' indicating HL7 version 2.3. Verified in task.json metadata test_messages field.
Extracted from HL7 PID-3 field (Patient ID): MRN10045^^^HOSPITAL1^MR. First component is MRN10045.
Extracted from HL7 PID-3 field: MRN20078^^^HOSPITAL1^MR. First component is MRN20078.
setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 98 uses docker image 'nextgenhealthcare/connect:4.5.0'. NextGen Healthcare Connect (formerly Mirth Connect) is a real healthcare integration engine product.

MRN values follow artificial pattern (MRN10045, MRN20078, MRN30112). SSN values are obviously fake: 123456789, 987654321, 456789123 are too simple/sequential. Phone numbers use 555 exchange (2175551234, 3125559876, 3095554567) which is standard for fake numbers. Patient names are realistic but generic.

privileged_info_summary: The task implements an HL7 ADT (Admission, Discharge, Transfer) to CSV conversion channel using NextGen Connect 4.5.0. Port 6661 is the standard MLLP protocol port for HL7 v2 messages. Test data is synthetic: MRN identifiers follow a clear pattern, SSN values (123456789, 987654321, 456789123) are obviously fake, and phone numbers use the 555 exchange standard for fake numbers. Both HL7 v2.3 test messages extract correctly to the expected CSV format with headers PatientID,LastName,FirstName,DOB,Gender,Street,City,State,Zip,Phone,SSN. The expected CSV outputs match the parsed patient demographics from the PID segments.
